label: Upper back contusion without spine or neurologic red flags
condition_terms: upper back contusion no spine red flags
upper back bruise no spine red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit an upper back bruise.
found: Your clinician diagnosed an upper back bruise. We did not find signs of spine injury, fracture, nerve injury, infection, or poor blood flow today.
home: Use ice wrapped in a cloth for short periods.
Return to normal activity gradually as pain improves.
meds: Use acetaminophen or ibuprofen only if you can take it safely and follow the label.
Use medicines only as prescribed or recommended.
return: New weakness, numbness, trouble walking, loss of bladder or bowel control, or severe worsening pain.
Fever, spreading redness, or new swelling.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, orthopedics, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated upper back contusion.
No spine injury concern, fracture concern, neurologic deficit, infection, or high-energy trauma.
exclusion: Midline spine tenderness, fracture or dislocation concern, neurologic deficit, high-energy trauma, infection concern, or specialist-directed spine plan.
high_confidence_terms: upper back contusion no spine red flags
upper back bruise no spine red flags
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus bruise material supports contusion self-care and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, or concerning symptoms. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed upper back contusion with spine, fracture, neurologic, infection, and high-energy trauma red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of upper back contusion

label: Trunk contusion without fracture or cardiopulmonary red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit a trunk bruise.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a trunk bruise. We did not find signs of fracture, dangerous chest or belly injury, infection, or poor blood flow today.
home: Rest the sore area as needed.
Use ice wrapped in a cloth for short periods.
Take deep breaths normally unless your clinician gave different instructions.
meds: Use acetaminophen or ibuprofen only if you can take it safely and follow the label.
Use medicines only as prescribed or recommended.
return: Chest pain, trouble breathing, fainting, coughing blood, or worsening belly pain.
New numbness, weakness, severe worsening pain, or fever.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated trunk contusion.
No fracture concern, cardiopulmonary red flags, abdominal red flags, infection, or high-energy trauma.
exclusion: Fracture or dislocation concern, chest pain, shortness of breath, abdominal pain red flags, high-energy trauma, unstable vital signs, or specialist-directed trauma plan.
must_not_miss: Pulmonary embolism or acute coronary syndrome when chest symptoms are present.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus bruise material supports contusion self-care and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, or concerning symptoms. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed trunk contusion with fracture, cardiopulmonary, abdominal, and high-energy trauma red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of trunk contusion
no chest pain or shortness of breath

label: Localized flea bites without infection or anaphylaxis
summary: Your symptoms fit a local skin reaction from flea bites.
found: Your clinician diagnosed localized flea bites. We did not find signs of anaphylaxis, serious allergic reaction, spreading infection, or a bite emergency today.
home: Wash the area gently with soap and water.
Use cool compresses for itching or swelling.
meds: Use itch or allergy medicines only if prescribed or recommended.
Use acetaminophen or ibuprofen only if you can take it safely and follow the label.
return: Trouble breathing, throat swelling, vomiting after a bite, fainting, or a bodywide reaction.
Spreading redness, pus, fever, red streaks, or worsening warmth.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized flea bites.
No anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, secondary infection, necrotic spider bite, tick-borne illness concern, or snakebite concern.
exclusion: Anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, secondary infection, necrotic spider or scorpion concern, tick-borne illness concern, snakebite concern, immunocompromised host, or specialist-directed allergy plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus insect bite material supports local bite care, itch control, and escalation for allergic reactions, infection, or systemic symptoms. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized flea bites with anaphylaxis and infection red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of localized flea bites
